title: Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb

seo.title: How to Make Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb — Easy Turkish Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb. This easy Turkish recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

easy Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb recipe
authentic Turkish Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb

h2Headings: What is Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb?

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Turkish Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb — a beloved Turkish classic

description: Kuzu Tandir is a timeless Turkish dish featuring lamb slow-roasted to incredible tenderness, practically falling off the bone. This method ensures the meat is succulent and deeply flavorful, absorbing the aromatic spices and juices. It is a true centerpiece for any special meal, beloved for its comforting and rich taste.

instructions.title: Prepare the Lamb
instructions.text: Pat the lamb dry with paper towels. Season generously all over with salt and black pepper. If desired, make a few shallow slits in the lamb and insert garlic slices for extra flavor, or simply place smashed garlic cloves around the lamb.

instructions.title: Sear the Lamb
instructions.text: Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven or a heavy roasting pan over medium-high heat. Sear the lamb on all sides until nicely browned. This step creates a flavorful crust and locks in juices.

instructions.title: Add Aromatics and Liquid
instructions.text: Remove the lamb temporarily. Add the quartered onions and remaining smashed garlic (if not inserted into the lamb) to the pot and saute for 2-3 minutes until slightly softened and fragrant. Return the lamb to the pot. Add the water or lamb broth and bay leaves and oregano, if using.

instructions.title: Cover and Start Roasting
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 160°C (325°F). Cover the Dutch oven tightly with its lid or seal the roasting pan securely with aluminum foil. Place it in the preheated oven.

instructions.title: Slow Roast to Perfection
instructions.text: Roast for 3 to 4 hours, or until the lamb is incredibly tender and easily pulls apart with a fork. The exact time will depend on the size and cut of your lamb. Check periodically to ensure there is enough liquid; add more hot water if necessary to prevent drying out.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the lamb from the oven and let it rest, still covered, for at least 15-20 minutes before serving. This allows the juices to redistribute, resulting in a more succulent meat. Shred the lamb directly in the pot or transfer it to a serving platter. Serve hot with its pan juices.

tips: For the most authentic Tandir flavor, consider adding a few dried red peppers (isot biber) to the roasting liquid for a subtle smoky heat.
Using bone-in lamb is crucial as the bones contribute significantly to the depth of flavor and moisture of the slow-cooked meat.
Kuzu Tandir is traditionally served with pilaf (rice pilaf) or bulgur pilaf, and often with a side of yogurt or a simple green salad to balance the richness.
article: Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb is a beloved Turkish Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Turkey. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
